strengths: Deep production TypeScript and Node.js experience confirmed across Optilyz (TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB) and re:cinq (TypeScript, Node.js, fullstack)
Active AI-agent infrastructure work at current role: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a direct match to the AI agent development requirement
Strong, demonstrated testing culture: TDD practitioner at RIDE Capital, authored D-language testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught TDD and Selenium workshops publicly
concerns: NestJS is absent from the entire resume — it is a named primary framework for this role with no evidence of exposure
Vitest is not mentioned; testing experience centers on custom D-lang tooling and Jest-adjacent practices, not the specified primary testing framework
Kotlin is missing from skills and employment history; Java is listed in skills but without any production backend Kotlin evidence, the secondary stack requirement is only half-met
response.justification: Bogdan presents 10+ years of fullstack experience with confirmed production TypeScript and Node.js work at Optilyz and re:cinq, satisfying the primary stack's language and runtime requirements. His current role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — a direct hit on a differentiating requirement that many candidates will lack. Cloud and containerization are evidenced by AWS CDK at Optilyz and Kubernetes deployment in GISCollective. The primary gaps are NestJS (unmentioned across 15+ years of history) and Vitest (also absent), both of which are named primary-stack items; however, given his breadth of framework adoption across many stacks and his strong architectural instincts, these represent a short ramp-up risk rather than a structural disqualifier. Kotlin is a secondary-stack gap but lower-stakes. Score derived from: strong primary-language alignment and AI-agent match (pushes up), offset by two named framework gaps (pulls down moderately).
